description: Environment setup: start Docker Postgres, run Prisma migration to create tables, verify existing build and tests pass with database available.
steps: Start a Docker Postgres container: docker run -d --name qi-pg -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=qi -e POSTGRES_USER=qi -e POSTGRES_DB=qi_platform -p 5432:5432 postgres:16-alpine (remove existing container first if needed)
Wait for Postgres to be ready (pg_isready or a SELECT 1 loop)
Run npx prisma migrate dev --name init in server/ to create all tables from the existing schema.prisma
Run npm run build to confirm prisma generate + tsc still work
Run npm test to confirm existing health tests still pass
acceptance_criteria: Docker Postgres is running and accepting connections on port 5432
Prisma migration creates all tables (User, Session, Membership, Tenant, Case, Project, etc.)
npm run build succeeds without errors
npm test passes all existing health tests
verification: docker exec qi-pg pg_isready -U qi returns success
npx prisma migrate status shows migration applied
findings: Docker container networking does not work in this sandbox (bwrap --unshare-pid with cgroup issues). Native Postgres must be installed via yum (Amazon Linux 2023). Postgres must be started within the SAME shell command that uses it (each execute_bash invocation is a separate bwrap session, so background processes do not persist). Use: su postgres -c 'postgres -D /projects/sandbox/.pg_data -k /projects/sandbox/.pg_run' & then run commands in the same shell invocation. Data dir at /projects/sandbox/.pg_data, socket at /projects/sandbox/.pg_run. DB user: qi, password: qi, database: qi_platform. Prisma migration created at server/prisma/migrations/20260614001630_init/. Build (npm run build) and tests (npm test) both pass without a running database. For future features needing DB access in tests, start Postgres inline within the test command execution.
